item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. It has the faintest shadow of an unimaginable horror that would really benefit from another skip/tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It still looks a little awkward. Try adding a line separating the header from the containment procedures because it looks off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appreciate the thoughts! For now, I edited the Nu-7 line to include a squadron rather than implying the whole MTF or anything so extreme as that. I'll continue to digest the rest of your feedback; thanks for your time, reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, you were very right. I've adjusted the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I feel like the position of the image would look much better if it was put below the header. It looks really awkward and makes the whole thing ugly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this was your first attempt at cosmic Horror then you definitely succeeded more than most. The only nitpick I have with that part of the story is that it wasn't particularly scary to me in the way most of the Lovecraft works I read were. It just lacked that moment where a glimpse of the horrific and unimaginable is revealed in my opinion. This might have been the blunt force trauma but it just didn't do it for me enough.

And I found it a bit excessive to use MTF Nu-7 ("Hammer Down") since there isn't really anything in the article to warrant use of a full battalion strength special force with tanks and the like.

It still was a very enjoyable read though. It kept my attention and my interest until the very end and left me wanting more about it. The release of the D-Class to normal society was a very nice touch that I haven't seen much on this site before.

+1

item.title: SCP-4401
item.content:encoded:
Hello, and welcome to my 2nd SCP.

The image can be found here at Pixabay.com and is shareable by the "Pixabay License" and Creative Commons.

A little taste of cosmic horror that I am looking forward to doing more with in the future.
